steps.title: Extract y from the simpler equation
steps.content:
From the second equation $5x = 2y$, divide both sides by 2:
$$y = \frac{5x}{2}$$

steps.title: Substitute into the first equation and solve
steps.content:
Substitute $y = \frac{5x}{2}$ into $y - 9x = 13$:
$$\frac{5x}{2} - 9x = 13$$
Multiply both sides by 2 to clear the fraction:
$$5x - 18x = 26$$
$$-13x = 26$$
$$x = -2$$

Back-substitute to find $y$:
$$y = \frac{5(-2)}{2} = \frac{-10}{2} = -5$$

The solution is $(-2, -5)$, which matches choice C — C is correct.
